title: oxalis psammophila
intro: Oxalis psammophila, also known as the sand-verbena oxalis, is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae) that is native to the southern parts of Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a diuretic and a laxative, as well as its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Oxalis psammophila include its potential to treat a range of conditions, such as kidney stones, urinary tract infections, and inflammation, as well as its ability to support digestive health. Oxalis psammophila can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is sometimes used in traditional herbal medicine in the form of a decoction or infusion.
